beats.text: Karen taps his elbow. Quiet. Pulls him behind the garage where nobody can see.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: I'm not going to yell. I'm not going to cry. I'm done with both.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: I'm calling my mom's lawyer Monday, David. That's it. That's the whole conversation.
Dad's world narrows to a pinhole. Somewhere past the fence, a kid is laughing. It sounds like it's from another life.

end_a.text: Karen walks back to the party. Dad stays behind the garage. A spider works, diligent, above his head.

end_b.text: Karen stops. Turns. Looks at him like he's a stranger she almost recognizes. Then walks back to the party.
